title: Deploy and Expose Nginx
description:
## Deploy and Expose an Nginx Application

The platform team needs a simple web server running in the cluster.

**Your tasks:**
1. Create a **Deployment** named `webserver` in the `default` namespace
2. Use the image `nginx:1.25`
3. Set replica count to **2**
4. Expose it via a **ClusterIP Service** named `webserver-svc` on port **80**

> 💡 Tip: You can use `kubectl create` for both resources imperatively, or write YAML manifests.

hints.title: Create the Deployment
hints.body: Use `kubectl create deployment` with the `--image` and `--replicas` flags.

hints.title: Expose the Deployment
hints.body: Use `kubectl expose` to create a ClusterIP service targeting port 80.

hints.title: Verify
hints.body: Check that both resources are up and the service endpoints are populated.
hints.command: kubectl get deployment webserver && kubectl get svc webserver-svc && kubectl get endpoints webserver-svc

validation.description: Checks that the deployment exists with 2 replicas, uses the correct image, and the service exists targeting port 80.
